Primary Use: AI-first website builder with Wireframer & Workshop - generates responsive layouts and interactive components from prompts
Superpower: Minutes to live website: AI generates production-ready layouts with built-in analytics, A/B testing, and mobile-first design
Limitations: Learning curve for advanced features; pricing scales with site complexity; best for design-first approach
Pricing: Free plan available; Mini $5/mo; Basic $15/mo; Pro $25/mo

Primary Use: AI-powered site builder with design system generation, smart responsiveness, and accessibility checking
Superpower: Design system automation: AI suggests colors, typography, buttons optimized for UI/UX standards with accessibility built-in
Limitations: Can be expensive for advanced features; AI in beta; requires Webflow familiarity for deep customization

Primary Use: AI sitemap & wireframe generator with component library - exports to Webflow/Figma/React
Superpower: Instant sitemap & wireframes: Exports to multiple platforms with AI copywriting and ready-to-use component library
Limitations: Not a hosted builder - requires export to Webflow/Figma; focuses on planning phase, not full implementation

Primary Use: Full IDE built on VS Code with AI integration - supports Claude 3.5, GPT-4o, GPT-5. Multi-file editing with Composer
Superpower: Superior codebase context: Understands entire project with Composer for multi-file edits, model flexibility (GPT-5, Claude 4.5)
Limitations: Editor flexibility limited (Cursor only); $20/month price point; developing enterprise compliance

Primary Use: AI UI generator by Vercel - creates React components with Tailwind CSS and ShadCN from natural language prompts
Superpower: React + Tailwind mastery: Instant UI generation with real-time iteration, publishes to shareable URLs
Limitations: Front-end only - no backend generation; requires Next.js knowledge for complex apps; iteration can be slow
Pricing: Free tier with limited generations; Premium plans for unlimited

Primary Use: AI coding assistant in Replit cloud IDE - plans, writes, runs code, manages dependencies, deploys apps
Superpower: End-to-end automation: Installs dependencies, manages files, deploys apps - true agentic coding assistant
Limitations: Cloud IDE dependency; limited to languages Replit supports; agentic features still evolving

Primary Use: Code generation with visual interface - edit, comment, port to different languages, review code inline
Superpower: Visual code editing: Edit inline, port languages, review code, explain specific lines - integrated canvas interface
Limitations: Requires ChatGPT Plus subscription; canvas feature still in beta; limited to supported languages

Primary Use: AI for code generation, debugging, refactoring - excels at explaining complex code and following detailed specs
Superpower: Code explanation expert: Best for understanding complex code, detailed specifications, natural language to code
Limitations: Not as specialized for web dev as Cursor; requires iterative prompting for complex tasks

Primary Use: AI autocomplete & chat for coding - excellent GitHub integration, works across multiple editors
Superpower: GitHub ecosystem king: Seamless integration with GitHub workflows, PR assistance, works in multiple editors
Limitations: Tied to Microsoft models; limited multi-file context vs Cursor; basic terminal integration

Primary Use: Browser-based AI development with WebContainers - scaffold full-stack apps from prompts, no local setup
Superpower: Zero setup required: Full-stack development in browser with WebContainers, offline work, instant deployment
Limitations: JavaScript/Node.js focused; complex non-JS stacks unsupported; rapid prototyping strength but not production-scale
Pricing: Free tier; Paid plans for advanced features

Primary Use: Chat-based AI platform for building web apps - direct Figma integration, natural language to functional apps
Superpower: Figma to app instantly: Direct import of Figma designs to functional apps via chat, enterprise-ready
Limitations: Relatively new platform; enterprise features still developing; best for internal tools & prototypes
Pricing: Free to start; Pro plans available

Primary Use: AI image generation for web design assets - abstract images, UI elements, backgrounds, stock photography
Superpower: Premium web assets: Generates abstract backgrounds, UI elements, icons, vector illustrations for high-end designs
Limitations: Requires prompt engineering skill; subscription cost; can't generate exact brand-specific assets

Primary Use: AI image generator by OpenAI - creates visuals from text prompts, integrated with ChatGPT
Superpower: Integrated creation: Built into ChatGPT, creates UI mockups, logos, blog images, social media graphics
Limitations: Only via ChatGPT Plus/Team; sometimes adds unwanted text to images; needs iteration for perfect results

Primary Use: AI copywriting with workflow automation - generates blog posts, ads, emails, social media content
Superpower: Workflow automation: API integration with no-code tools, creates blog outlines and social hooks automatically
Limitations: Learning curve for automation features; requires API integration for advanced workflows

Primary Use: AI marketing copy generator - templates for landing pages, ads, blogs, brand voice customization
Superpower: Marketing focus: Landing page creator, team collaboration, brand voice guides, full campaign copy
Limitations: Higher price point; can produce generic copy without brand voice training; needs human editing

Primary Use: AI writing assistant in Notion - generates blog posts, outlines, summaries, SEO headlines
Superpower: Native integration: Works directly in Notion workspace, SEO headlines, blog posts, content summarization
Limitations: Limited to text content; can't generate images natively; requires Notion workspace

Primary Use: Figma/XD to code converter using Large Design Models - exports React, Vue, HTML with component mapping
Superpower: Large Design Models: Trained on millions of designs, automatically maps custom components, smart suggestions
Limitations: Export code needs cleanup; works best with design systems; Figma plugin dependency

Primary Use: Design-to-code platform - converts Figma to HTML, CSS, React, Vue with responsive design
Superpower: Multi-framework export: HTML, React, Vue - drag-and-drop editor with custom code scripts

Primary Use: Figma to React/HTML exporter - generates developer-friendly code with props and components
Superpower: Component-level export: Copy React code for specific elements, works with design systems

Primary Use: AI wireframe & UI generator Figma plugin - text-to-UI with editable frames
Superpower: Rapid prototyping: Generates wireframes and high-fidelity screens from prompts or references
Limitations: Figma plugin dependency; may need design refinement; Premium tier for unlimited generations

Primary Use: Design copilot for Figma - generates app UI from text, mixes components from other apps
Superpower: Component mixing: Combines elements from existing apps to create unique interfaces in minutes
Limitations: Premium features $20/month; requires understanding of UI patterns; outputs need designer review

Primary Use: ChatGPT & DALL-E integration for Figma - AI assistance directly in design workflow
Superpower: Dual AI power: Access both ChatGPT text and DALL-E images within Figma design workflow
